title: Choose and Choose Again
summary: A commencement address at the College of Western Idaho. Daly recounts her personal story—dropping out of high school, earning a GED, and finding economics—to encourage graduates that paths are not predetermined. It is a ceremonial, motivational speech with no content on inflation, the labor market, the policy stance, or the rate path. Non-policy for reaction-function purposes.

key_quotes.quote: The truth is, decisions are rarely final and paths are rarely predetermined.
key_quotes.context: Central motivational message of the commencement address; no monetary-policy content.

key_quotes.quote: And we all have the opportunity to choose and choose again.
key_quotes.context: Title theme of the ceremonial speech.
